beats.label: The Greyback's Departure (or Brynja's Last Room)
beats.desc: Establish the uneasy atmosphere of leaving Törweg and receiving the note naming Mathr. Aboard the Greyback, heading north, with the name "Mathr" weighing heavily. (Or, departing Brynja's home, feeling watched.)

beats.label: The Ferryman’s Tale
beats.desc: Introduce a seemingly harmless encounter that reveals the subtle control exerted by Mathr’s influence. Continuing north, with a reinforced sense of the pervasive influence of Mathr.

beats.label: The Roadside Inn
beats.desc: Provide a chance for rest and information gathering, but with the risk of being overheard or manipulated. Leaving the inn with a sense of being watched and a lingering suspicion about the innkeeper’s motives.

beats.label: The Market Inspection
beats.desc: reinforce the growing sense of Vishara's influence, and subtly hint at Mathr’s longevity while creating a tense social encounter. This encounter forces the party to confront the consequences of their actions and highlights the increasing scrutiny they are under. The party is quickly cleared and gains access to Duvash, learning his message

beats.label: The Echo of Witness
beats.desc: Ragnfridd's Skaldic identity is tested, highlighting the corruption of truth traditions. Leaving the village with a deeper understanding of how truth can be manipulated and suppressed through tradition.

beats.label: Mountain Ambush
beats.desc: To directly advance the "Pryzi key — gone east" hot thread by forcing a confrontation with Shadow-Walkers attempting to retrieve the Pryzi manifest and bone disc from the party. This encounter also introduces a tangible threat related to the Pryzi vault and hints at the wider scope of Vishara's influence. The party are reminded this is bigger than just Lösweg

scenes.label: 1 · The Greyback's Departure (or Brynja's Last Room)
scenes.meta: Chapter chapters/the-weight-of-witness · Törweg Harbour (or Brynja’s home)
scenes.title: The Greyback's Departure (or Brynja's Last Room)
scenes.summary: Establish the uneasy atmosphere of leaving Törweg and receiving the note naming Mathr. Aboard the Greyback, heading north, with the name "Mathr" weighing heavily. (Or, departing Brynja's home, feeling watched.)

blocks.text: The Greyback's Departure (or Brynja's Last Room)

blocks.bodyHtml:
Location: Törweg Harbour (or Brynja’s home)
Purpose: Establish the uneasy atmosphere of leaving Törweg and receiving the note naming Mathr.
Pressure: The unsettling normalcy of the departure; the lack of overt obstruction.

blocks.heading: Choices
itemsHtml: Depart quietly, investigate the Truthspeaker’s actions, attempt to gather more information in Törweg.

blocks.heading: Clues
itemsHtml: Observing subtle signs of surveillance or unusual behaviour amongst the townspeople.

blocks.label: Failure
blocks.bodyHtml: Being subtly warned off by a seemingly innocuous individual, losing the opportunity to gather more information.

blocks.label: Exit State
blocks.bodyHtml: Aboard the Greyback, heading north, with the name "Mathr" weighing heavily. (Or, departing Brynja's home, feeling watched.)

actions.label: The Greyback's Departure (or Brynja's Last Room)

scenes.label: 2 · The Ferryman’s Tale
scenes.meta: Chapter chapters/the-weight-of-witness · A river ferry crossing on the route to Valweg.
scenes.title: The Ferryman’s Tale
scenes.summary: Introduce a seemingly harmless encounter that reveals the subtle control exerted by Mathr’s influence. Continuing north, with a reinforced sense of the pervasive influence of Mathr.

blocks.bodyHtml:
Location: A river ferry crossing on the route to Valweg.
Purpose: Introduce a seemingly harmless encounter that reveals the subtle control exerted by Mathr’s influence.
Pressure: The ferryman’s evasive answers and veiled warnings about “trouble” and “respecting authority.”

blocks.heading: Choices
itemsHtml: Press the ferryman for information, attempt to subtly probe his knowledge of Mathr, ignore his warnings and proceed.

blocks.heading: Clues
itemsHtml: The ferryman’s discomfort and carefully chosen words, hints of local fear and obedience.

blocks.label: Failure
blocks.bodyHtml: Offending the ferryman, delaying their progress, attracting unwanted attention.

blocks.label: Exit State
blocks.bodyHtml: Continuing north, with a reinforced sense of the pervasive influence of Mathr.

scenes.label: 3 · The Roadside Inn
scenes.meta: Chapter chapters/the-weight-of-witness · A small, isolated inn along the road to Valweg.
scenes.title: The Roadside Inn
scenes.summary: Provide a chance for rest and information gathering, but with the risk of being overheard or manipulated. Leaving the inn with a sense of being watched and a lingering suspicion about the innkeeper’s motives.

blocks.bodyHtml:
Location: A small, isolated inn along the road to Valweg.
Purpose: Provide a chance for rest and information gathering, but with the risk of being overheard or manipulated.
Pressure: The innkeeper’s overly friendly demeanor and probing questions about their purpose.

blocks.heading: Choices
itemsHtml: Be forthcoming with information, be guarded and evasive, attempt to gather information from other patrons.

blocks.heading: Clues
itemsHtml: Overheard conversations hinting at Mathr’s power and influence, subtle signs of surveillance.

blocks.label: Failure
blocks.bodyHtml: Being subtly manipulated into revealing information, attracting unwanted attention from local authorities.

blocks.label: Exit State
blocks.bodyHtml: Leaving the inn with a sense of being watched and a lingering suspicion about the innkeeper’s motives.

scenes.label: 4 · The Market Inspection
scenes.title: The Market Inspection
scenes.summary: reinforce the growing sense of Vishara's influence, and subtly hint at Mathr’s longevity while creating a tense social encounter. This encounter forces the party to confront the consequences of their actions and highlights the increasing scrutiny they are under. The party is quickly cleared and gains access to Duvash, learning his message

blocks.bodyHtml:
Location: Akrafjall
Purpose: reinforce the growing sense of Vishara's influence, and subtly hint at Mathr’s longevity while creating a tense social encounter. This encounter forces the party to confront the consequences of their actions and highlights the increasing scrutiny they are under.
Pressure: Guard Captain Brynn wants to delay the party’s access to Duvash, subtly probe their cargo for anything unusual, and assess their overall demeanor. He's acting under pressure from an unknown source (Vane) to keep a close eye on activity in the market. He’s not looking for outright contraband, but for signs of disruption or illicit connections.

blocks.heading: Choices
itemsHtml: Comply, Challenge, Bribe, Split Group, Distract, Follow

blocks.heading: Clues
itemsHtml: The seal on the document is the Mathr sigil, The questions are too specific for a routine inspection, They are not interested in the ordinary cargo.

blocks.label: Failure
blocks.bodyHtml: The party is detained, delaying their progress and potentially alerting Vane to their interest in Duvash.

blocks.label: Exit State
blocks.bodyHtml: The party is quickly cleared and gains access to Duvash, learning his message

scenes.label: 5 · The Echo of Witness
scenes.meta: Chapter chapters/the-weight-of-witness · A small village known for its traditional Skaldic practices.
scenes.title: The Echo of Witness
scenes.summary: Ragnfridd's Skaldic identity is tested, highlighting the corruption of truth traditions. Leaving the village with a deeper understanding of how truth can be manipulated and suppressed through tradition.

blocks.bodyHtml:
Location: A small village known for its traditional Skaldic practices.
Purpose: Ragnfridd's Skaldic identity is tested, highlighting the corruption of truth traditions.
Pressure: The village’s reverence for tradition clashes with the party's pursuit of truth, creating a sense of dissonance.

blocks.heading: Choices
itemsHtml: Engage with the village Skalds, challenge their traditional narratives, seek guidance from the village elder.

blocks.heading: Clues
itemsHtml: Discrepancies between the official Skaldic accounts and the villagers' whispered fears, a sense of suppressed dissent.

blocks.label: Failure
blocks.bodyHtml: Offending the village Skalds, alienating the villagers, losing the opportunity to gain valuable insights.

blocks.label: Exit State
blocks.bodyHtml: Leaving the village with a deeper understanding of how truth can be manipulated and suppressed through tradition.

This encounter also introduces a tangible threat related to the Pryzi vault and hints at the wider scope of Vishara's influence. The party are reminded this is bigger than just Lösweg

blocks.bodyHtml:
Location: Just south of Valweg
Purpose: To directly advance the "Pryzi key — gone east" hot thread by forcing a confrontation with Shadow-Walkers attempting to retrieve the Pryzi manifest and bone disc from the party. This encounter also introduces a tangible threat related to the Pryzi vault and hints at the wider scope of Vishara's influence.
Pressure: Threat and Combat.

blocks.heading: Clues
itemsHtml: A coded message, another bone disc.

blocks.label: Failure
blocks.bodyHtml: Loss of the manifest and bone disc, possible casualties

blocks.label: Exit State
blocks.bodyHtml: The party are reminded this is bigger than just Lösweg
